title: Granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor/elastin-like polypeptide fusion proteins and uses thereof
abstract: The disclosure provides for soluble, biologically active fusion proteins comprising granulocyte-macrophage colony- stimulating factor (GM-CSF) that has been fused or linked to a polypeptide capable of undergoing reversible temperature-dependent phase separation, and uses thereof.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED: 1. A fusion protein comprising (a) a granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM- CSF) ; and (b) a polypeptide that undergoes reversible temperature- dependent phase separation, wherein the GM-CSF is operably linked to the polypeptide that undergoes reversible temperature-dependent phase separation and wherein the fusion protein is soluble in aqueous solutions and exhibits the biological properties of native GM-CSF. 2. The fusion protein of claim 1, wherein the GM-CSF is human GM-CSF, canine GM-CSF, feline GM-CSF, murine GM-CSF, or non-human primate GM-CSF. 3. The fusion protein of claim 1, wherein the GM-CSF is human GM-CSF. 4. The fusion protein of any one of claim 1, 2 or 3, wherein the polypeptide allows for direct purification of the fusion protein from the soluble fraction of a microbial lysate. 5. The fusion protein of claim 1, 2, or 3, wherein the fusion protein is isolated from E. coli. 6. The fusion protein of claim 1, 2 or 3, wherein the fusion protein has a median phase transition temperature of about 15 Â°C, about 16 Â°C, about 17 Â°C, about 18 Â°C, about 19 Â°C, about 20 Â°C, about 21 Â°C, about 22 Â°C, about 23 Â°C, about 24 Â°C, about 25 Â°C, about 26 Â°C, about 27 Â°C, about 28 Â°C, about 29 Â°C, about 30 Â°C, about 31 Â°C, about 32 Â°C, about 33 Â°C, about 34 Â°C, about 35 Â°C, about 36 Â°C, about 37 Â°C, about 38 Â°C, about 39 Â°C, about 40 Â°C, about 41 Â°C, about 42 Â°C, about 43 Â°C, about 44 Â°C, about 45 Â°C, about 46 Â°C, about 47 Â°C, or about 48 Â°C. 7. The fusion protein of claim 1, wherein the polypeptide is an elastin-like polypeptide (ELP) . 8. The fusion protein of claim 7, wherein the ELP has a sequence comprising (VPGWG; SEQ ID NO:8) n , (VPGYG; SEQ ID NO:9) n , (VPGFG; SEQ ID NO : 10 ) n , (VPGIG; SEQ ID NO:5) n , (VPGLG; SEQ ID NO:6) n , (VPGVG; SEQ ID NO : 4 ) n, (VPGAG; SEQ ID NO:3) n, (VPGGG; SEQ ID NO:ll) n, (VPGSG; SEQ ID NO : 12 ) n, (VPGTG; SEQ ID NO:13) n, (VPGMG; SEQ ID NO:14) n, (VPGNG; SEQ ID NO:15) n, (VPGQG; SEQ ID NO:16) n, (VPGHG; SEQ ID NO: 17) n, (VPGKG; SEQ ID NO:18) n, (VPGRG; SEQ ID NO:19) n (VPGDG; SEQ ID NO : 20 ) n , (VPGEG; SEQ ID NO:21) n, (VPGCG; SEQ ID NO:22) n , where n is an integer selected from 1 to 500. 9. The fusion protein of claim 8, wherein the ELP has a sequence comprising ELP-A192. 10. The fusion protein of claim 1, 2 or 3, wherein the fusion proteins are isolated from prokaryotic or eukaryotic cells as nanoparticles . 11. The fusion protein of claim 10, wherein the fusion protein nanoparticles have an average hydrodynamic radius from 30 nm to 60 nm. 12. The fusion protein of claim 1, wherein the fusion protein further comprises one or more spacer or linker domains. 13. The fusion protein of claim 12, wherein the one or more spacer or linker domains separates the GM-CSF domain and the polypeptide domain. 14. The fusion protein of claim 1, wherein the fusion protein comprises, from the amino-terminus to the carboxyl-terminus , GM- CSF, optionally a spacer or linker domain, the polypeptide that undergoes reversible temperature-dependent phase separation, optionally a linker domain and optionally a cell targeting domain or a membrane translocation domain. 15. The fusion protein of claim 1, wherein the fusion protein exhibits one or more of the following advantages over recombinant human GMCSF : reduced renal clearance; better suited for targeting bone marrow; promotes sustained delivery following a subcutaneous inj ection; can be tailored to enhance local engraftment of blood-forming cells ; less toxicity for subjects with yeast allergies; and can be used effectively at lower dosing. 16. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a fusion polypeptide of claim 1 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 17. An isolated polynucleotide encoding the fusion polypeptide of claim 1. 18. A vector comprising the polynucleotide of claim 17. 19. A host cell containing the vector of claim 18. 20. A host cell comprising the polynucleotide of claim 17. 21. A method of treating a subject with lower than normal levels of neutrophils, monocytes /macrophages , myeloid-derived dendritic cells, and/or lower than normal levels of activated mature granulocytes and macrophages comprising: administering to the subject an effective amount of a fusion protein of claim 1 or a pharmaceutical composition made therefrom. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the subject was previously treated with chemotherapy and/or had a bone marrow transplantation. 23. The method of claim 21, wherein the fusion protein is administered as a depot formulation by subcutaneous, intramuscular, or intraosseous administration. 24. The method of claim 21, wherein the fusion protein is administered intravenously. 25. Use of the fusion protein of clam 1 for treatment of an indication selected from the following: for use following induction chemotherapy in subjects with acute myelogenous leukemia (AML) ; for mobilization of hematopoietic progenitor cells into peripheral blood for collection by leukapheresis ; for myeloid reconstitution after autologous bone marrow transplantation (BMT) ; for myeloid reconstitution after allogeneic bone marrow transplantation; for use in bone marrow transplantation failure or engraftment delay; and for use in expanding myeloid cell lines for CART therapies.
